sessions.lever: v0/i split + xor intermediate + drop equal-branch sll + anti-cheat (0,...) comma removal

reviewer.evidence: candidate is net anti-cheat (removes (0,...) comma; merges duplicate equal-branch v0=s1<<1 into the shared do_sll path); v0/i split + named-xor-intermediate covered by SOTN-allowed families; no register pins, __asm__, volatile coercions, alias renames, unused arrays, dead-store params, or non-sanctioned wrapper constructs.

remaining_gap.summary: 4 real diffs survive ~25 C-level variants: (1) idx 25 commutative addu operand swap — `addu s2, s0, v0` vs target `addu s2, v0, s0` (1 insn); (2) idx 37/38/40 GCC loop-strength-reduction cluster — our build precomputes `v1 = s2 + 0x332` once before the loop and uses small displacement (2/0); target keeps `v1 = s2` and uses large displacement (820/818) (3 insns coupled). Branch addresses (idx 9/11/13/17/35/45/53/59) are masked-out and don't count.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: All 9 HEAD regfix rules can be retired ONCE the remaining 4 RTL-level diffs close — the rules are: 6 reg-renames in idx 18-25 (xor block: $4→$2 chain), and 3 strength-reduce offset-encodes in idx 37-40. Candidate already eliminates the need for the 6 reg-rename rules (sandbox-disable-all-clean for that cluster); only the 3 strength-reduce rules + the operand-swap remain (4 actual insn diffs).

next_hypotheses: Strength-reduce cluster: investigate whether ANY function in src/code6cac_b.c currently matches with a multi-displacement loop on a step-2 BIV. If none, the file may be an -fno-strength-reduce candidate — but that requires user policy sign-off per [[compiler-flags-canonical]] / [[no-compiler-divergence]] (flags frozen).

Strength-reduce cluster: try instrumented cc1 dump with BB2_LOOP_DEBUG / loop.c trace to see WHY our build's strength_reduce() decides the s2+0x332 GIV is profitable. Examine the benefit calc; identify the specific RTL state to flip.

Operand-swap idx 25: investigate whether commute_machine_combine flipping the operand order is feasible via a C-level lever (declaration order, intervening computation, ptr-type-cast on v0). v23/v11 attempts ADDED instructions; the swap may require RTL-level operand-precedence manipulation that pure C can't easily reach.

Run the decomp-permuter starting from candidate.c base (sandbox 4, not 9). Setup: build target.o from asm/funcs/cpu_check_tubazeri_2.s + tools/decomp-permuter/prelude.inc (drop `.set gp=64` for r3000). Workspace at permuter/cpu_check_tubazeri_2/. The permuter's randomization can find structural levers not in the manual playbook.
Cross-reference m2c output for cpu_check_tubazeri_2 to see what control-flow shape m2c reconstructs from target's asm — may reveal a different loop or addressing structure to try.

rejected_forms.form: block-local v0 declaration (Lever A applied to v0)
rejected_forms.reason: Breaks the equal-branch v0=s1<<1 dataflow; reordered the xor-block RA into a worse allocation.
GCC still strength-reduces. Worse than ptr-based by adding loop-prologue overhead.
Changes the loop's entry shape; GCC emits the loop body unconditionally with worse scheduling.
Same floor — GCC still strength-reduces with u16 ptr; only the GIV's stride constant changes (0x199*2=0x332).
GCC adds explicit conversion instructions; raises bi from 76 to 74 (deletes) + 4 substs = worse total.

rejected_forms.form: explicit s2_int intermediate before pointer cast
rejected_forms.reason: Same as above — adds insns rather than swapping operand order.

rejected_forms.form: for-loop with comma-init (`for (i=s1, ptr=s2; ...; i++, ptr=...)` )
rejected_forms.reason: Comma-init shifts the entry edge; GCC schedules the prologue setup differently and many idx-aligned diffs cascade.
